title: Declarative Parallel Dataflow with Future/Mirai/SLURM
text: Declarative parallel dataflow R — laptop HPC. Define compute, loop. Parade builds typed, parallel workflows; persists large outputs artifacts (sinks); talks SLURM directly (submit, monitor, cancel) rarely leave R. Website & docs: https://bbuchsbaum.github.io/parade/ Source: https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/parade parade? Clean, composable pipelines explicit types lazily persisted outputs, plus first-class HPC ergonomics (portable paths, SLURM defaults, live monitoring).

title: Declarative Parallel Dataflow with Future/Mirai/SLURM
text: Note: CRAN package named parade unrelated (economic “income parades”). project currently GitHub-.
code: # development version # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("bbuchsbaum/parade")

title: Declarative Parallel Dataflow with Future/Mirai/SLURM
text: Artifacts (sinks) keep memory tiny runs resumable — see Artifacts vignette. Portable paths like artifacts:// resolve scratch HPC temp laptops — see Smart Path Management. Typed returns (dbl(), int(), lst(), artifact()) catch mistakes early — see Core concepts.
code: library(parade) library(progressr) handlers(global = TRUE)   # progress bars everywhere  paths_init()              # portable paths: artifacts://, data://, etc.  # Declare the parameter space grid <- param_grid(subject = c("s01", "s02"), session = 1:2)  # Build a typed, composable pipeline fl <- flow(grid) |>   stage(     id = "fit",     f = function(subject, session) {       model <- lm(rnorm(1000) ~ rnorm(1000))       list(model = model, rmse = runif(1))     },     schema = schema(model = artifact(), rmse = dbl()),   # big → artifact, small → memory     sink   = sink_spec(fields = "model",                        dir = "artifacts://fits",                        template = "{.stage}/{subject}/ses{session}-{.row_key}")   )  # Execute locally or with futures/mirai/SLURM res <- collect(fl, engine = "future", workers = 4)  res$model[[1]]   # file-ref (path, bytes, sha256, written/existed) res$rmse         # numeric in-memory

what: Submit & monitor SLURM jobs from R
title: Declarative Parallel Dataflow with Future/Mirai/SLURM
text: Defaults & omit--NA built — see Using SLURM Defaults. script_top() / jobs_top() give text dashboard CPU/RSS + logs — see SLURM script submission & monitoring. Distribution options (grouping/barriers, throttling, chunking) declarative — see Distribution: local & SLURM.
code: # One-command HPC setup (recommended for clusters) parade_init_hpc(persist = TRUE)  slurm_defaults_set(   partition = "general",   time = "2h",           # accepts 2h / 120min / H:MM:SS   cpus_per_task = 8,   mem = NA,              # omit --mem if your site forbids it   persist = TRUE )  job <- submit_slurm("scripts/train.R", args = c("--fold", "1"))  parade_dashboard(job)  # unified summary (or action = "top" for live UI) script_status(job)     # quick check script_tail(job, 80) script_top(job)     # live CPU/RSS and logs  # Multiple jobs together: jobs_top(list(job1, job2, job3))

title: Declarative Parallel Dataflow with Future/Mirai/SLURM
text: Standard future/furrr parallelism capped ~125 connections. mirai backend lifts limit running persistent daemon workers pull tasks central dispatcher — giving low-latency fan-, automatic load balancing, optional SSH/TLS transport. See Mirai backend patterns tradeoffs.
code: # Local: spin up 8 daemon workers on this machine. # The dispatcher feeds tasks to whichever worker is free next. fl |>   distribute(dist_mirai(n = 8, dispatcher = TRUE)) |>   collect()  # HPC: launch 32 daemon workers as SLURM jobs. # Each worker is a persistent R process that pulls work from the dispatcher, # so you get load balancing across nodes without pre-partitioning the grid. handle <- fl |>   distribute(use_mirai_slurm(n = 32, partition = "compute", time = "2h")) |>   submit()

title: Declarative Parallel Dataflow with Future/Mirai/SLURM
text: Hard-coded paths break move laptop cluster. Parade solves protocol-style aliases resolve right directory machine: aliases — artifacts://, data://, scratch://, registry://, config://, cache:// — check environment variables first (PARADE_ARTIFACTS, PARADE_SCRATCH, …), fall back sensible defaults (shared scratch SLURM, tempdir locally). Override paths_set() parade_init_hpc(). See Smart Path Management.
code: sink_spec(fields = "model", dir = "artifacts://fits") #  on laptop → /tmp/parade-artifacts/fits #  on HPC    → $SCRATCH/parade-artifacts/fits

title: Declarative Parallel Dataflow with Future/Mirai/SLURM
code: # List artifacts under your artifacts root (uses sink sidecars when present) artifact_catalog()  # Search by stage/field/row_key/path substring artifact_catalog_search(query = "fit")

title: Declarative Parallel Dataflow with Future/Mirai/SLURM
text: Parade deliberately small compositional: - Dataframe-shaped param grids vs. global DAG caches - Pseudo-typed returns crisp contracts - Built-sinks large results - HPC ergonomics: SLURM submission, defaults, monitoring, path aliases play nicely together; parade focuses elegant, fast fan-/fan-.

what: Add an inline code block stage to a parade flow — code_stage
title: Add an inline code block stage to a parade flow — code_stage
text: code_stage() captures bare code block via non-standard evaluation wraps function whose formals automatically inferred grid columns upstream stage outputs. fills gap tiny lambdas passed stage() external scripts used script_stage(): code lives inline pipeline definition can span many lines without cluttering pipe.

title: Add an inline code block stage to a parade flow — code_stage
code: code_stage(   fl,   id,   code,   needs = character(),   schema,   prefix = TRUE,   sink = NULL,   skip_when = NULL,   hoist_struct = FALSE,   inputs = NULL,   input_artifacts = NULL,   outputs = NULL,   io_mode = c("off", "warn", "error"),   retries = NULL,   retry_backoff = NULL,   retry_base = NULL,   retry_on = NULL,   resources = NULL,   cpus = NULL,   memory = NULL,   time = NULL,   ... )

title: Add an inline code block stage to a parade flow — code_stage
text: fl parade_flow object id Unique stage identifier (character) code bare R expression (code block) execute row. Captured via base::substitute() — never evaluated caller's frame.  Grid columns upstream outputs injected local variables. needs Character vector stage IDs stage depends schema Schema defining expected output structure (returns()) prefix Whether prefix output columns stage ID (logical) sink Optional sink specification artifact persistence skip_when Optional function determine skip stage hoist_struct Whether hoist nested data structures (logical) inputs Optional character vector required input names stage. input_artifacts Optional character vector inputs must artifact/file-reference values. outputs Optional character vector declared output names. Defaults schema column names. io_mode /O contract behavior: "" (default), "warn", "error". retries Optional stage-level retry override (non-negative integer). retry_backoff Optional stage-level retry backoff override: "none", "fixed", "linear", "exponential". retry_base Optional stage-level retry base delay (seconds). retry_on Optional stage-level retry classifier override. resources Optional stage-level resource hints named list keys cpus, memory, time (aliases: cpus_per_task, ncpus, mem). cpus Optional stage-level CPUs per task hint. memory Optional stage-level memory hint. time Optional stage-level walltime hint. ... Additional constant arguments passed stage function

title: Add an inline code block stage to a parade flow — code_stage
text: input flow new stage added.

title: Add an inline code block stage to a parade flow — code_stage
text: code block placed right id reads naturally pipeline style needs schema named. Inside block, grid columns upstream outputs (prefixed <stage>.<field>) available plain variables. block return named list matching schema.

title: Add an inline code block stage to a parade flow — code_stage
code: grid <- data.frame(x = 1:3) fl <- flow(grid) |>   code_stage("sq", {     list(result = x^2)   }, schema = returns(result = dbl())) #> Error in .stage_available_cols(fl, needs): could not find function ".stage_available_cols"  # With upstream dependencies — code block at end with named args fl2 <- flow(grid) |>   stage("dbl", function(x) list(y = x * 2),         schema = returns(y = dbl())) |>   code_stage("add", needs = "dbl",              schema = returns(total = dbl()), code = {     total <- x + dbl.y     list(total = total)   }) #> Error in .stage_available_cols(fl, needs): could not find function ".stage_available_cols"

title: Create crew distribution specification — dist_crew
text: Configure distributed execution using crew ecosystem.

title: Create crew distribution specification — dist_crew
code: dist_crew(   controller = NULL,   by = NULL,   chunks_per_job = 1L,   target_jobs = NULL,   within = c("sequential", "multisession", "multicore", "callr"),   workers_within = NULL,   persist = FALSE,   stop_on_exit = TRUE )

title: Create crew distribution specification — dist_crew
text: controller crew controller object, function arguments returns one. NULL, local controller used available. Column names group parallelization. chunks_per_job Number groups process per crew task. target_jobs Optional integer; target number tasks create (overrides chunks_per_job submit time). within Execution strategy within crew task: "multisession", "multicore", "callr", "sequential". workers_within Number workers within crew task. persist Logical; whether keep underlying worker pool alive collecting/canceling. Default FALSE (safer semantics). stop_on_exit Logical; whether terminate workers automatically collecting results (persist = FALSE). Default TRUE.

title: Create local distribution specification — dist_local
text: Configure local parallel execution using future framework.

title: Create local distribution specification — dist_local
code: dist_local(   by = NULL,   within = c("multisession", "multicore", "callr", "sequential"),   workers_within = NULL,   chunks_per_job = 1L,   target_jobs = NULL,   callr_timeout = NULL )

title: Create local distribution specification — dist_local
text: Character vector grid column names used define groups. unique combination columns becomes one group, groups unit parallelism — group runs independent future. = "subject" 20 subjects → 20 groups = c("subject", "session") → one group per subject×session combo = NULL (default) → every row group within run work inside job (chunk). first three modes use furrr parallelise across rows; "callr" parallelises across groups, giving group independent R process. "sequential" (default): one row time, extra parallelism. Simplest, lowest memory. "multisession": spawn R sub-processes via furrr; rows distributed across workers. "multicore": forked processes via furrr (Linux/macOS , RStudio). Faster startup multisession, shares memory. "callr": group-level process pool. group runs fully independent R process via callr::r_bg(). workers_within processes run concurrently; one finishes next group launched (work-queue pattern). ideal packing node processes use internal parallelism (e.g., script calls furrr, mclapply, uses threaded BLAS/OpenMP). Parade manage internal parallelism — script/function free use many cores needs. workers_within Integer; many parallel workers ("multisession" / "multicore") concurrent R processes ("callr") run inside job. used "sequential". Defaults NULL (auto: uses parallelly::availableCores() SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK cluster node). chunks_per_job many groups pack single future. Defaults 1 (one group per future). Increase reduce scheduling overhead many small groups. chunks_per_job = 1: 100 groups → 100 futures chunks_per_job = 5: 100 groups → 20 futures (5 groups ) target_jobs Integer; total number futures create. Overrides chunks_per_job — parade divides groups evenly across many futures. Useful want fixed number parallel units regardless many groups exist. target_jobs = 10 100 groups → 10 futures (10 groups ) target_jobs = NULL (default): use chunks_per_job instead callr_timeout Numeric; per-process timeout seconds within = "callr". callr worker alive longer , killed result () recovered. Useful safety net stage functions may leave background processes prevent clean exit. NULL (default) means timeout.

title: Create local distribution specification — dist_local
text: parade_dist object local execution

title: Create local distribution specification — dist_local
code: # One future per group, rows run sequentially inside each dist_local(by = "group", within = "sequential") #> $backend #> [1] "local" #>  #> $by #> [1] "group" #>  #> $within #> [1] "sequential" #>  #> $workers_within #> NULL #>  #> $chunks_per_job #> [1] 1 #>  #> $target_jobs #> NULL #>  #> $callr_timeout #> NULL #>  #> $slurm #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "parade_dist"  # Same grouping, but use forked parallelism inside each group dist_local(by = "group", within = "multicore") #> $backend #> [1] "local" #>  #> $by #> [1] "group" #>  #> $within #> [1] "multicore" #>  #> $workers_within #> NULL #>  #> $chunks_per_job #> [1] 1 #>  #> $target_jobs #> NULL #>  #> $callr_timeout #> NULL #>  #> $slurm #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "parade_dist"  # Pack 2 groups per future to reduce overhead dist_local(by = "group", chunks_per_job = 2L) #> $backend #> [1] "local" #>  #> $by #> [1] "group" #>  #> $within #> [1] "multisession" #>  #> $workers_within #> NULL #>  #> $chunks_per_job #> [1] 2 #>  #> $target_jobs #> NULL #>  #> $callr_timeout #> NULL #>  #> $slurm #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "parade_dist"  # Fix at 4 futures regardless of group count dist_local(by = "group", target_jobs = 4L) #> $backend #> [1] "local" #>  #> $by #> [1] "group" #>  #> $within #> [1] "multisession" #>  #> $workers_within #> NULL #>  #> $chunks_per_job #> [1] 1 #>  #> $target_jobs #> [1] 4 #>  #> $callr_timeout #> NULL #>  #> $slurm #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "parade_dist"  # Process pool: run up to 4 groups concurrently as independent R processes. # Each process can use its own internal parallelism (furrr, mclapply, etc.) dist_local(by = "subject", within = "callr", workers_within = 4L) #> $backend #> [1] "local" #>  #> $by #> [1] "subject" #>  #> $within #> [1] "callr" #>  #> $workers_within #> [1] 4 #>  #> $chunks_per_job #> [1] 1 #>  #> $target_jobs #> NULL #>  #> $callr_timeout #> NULL #>  #> $slurm #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "parade_dist"

text: n Number local daemons launch url Listening URL remote connections (auto-generated NULL). May string zero-argument function returning string. remote Remote configuration mirai::daemons(). May config object returned mirai::ssh_config()/mirai::cluster_config(), zero-argument function returning config. dispatcher Use dispatcher automatic load balancing tls Use TLS encryption connections port Port number connections (auto-selected NULL) stop_on_exit Automatically cleanup daemons finished within Execution strategy within job: "mirai" "sequential" workers_within Number workers nested parallelization chunks_per_job Number groups process per job Column names group parallelization

code: # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("mirai", quietly = TRUE)) {   # Local daemons   dist_mirai(n = 4)      # With TLS encryption   dist_mirai(n = 8, tls = TRUE, port = 5555) } #> $backend #> [1] "mirai" #>  #> $by #> character(0) #>  #> $n #> [1] 8 #>  #> $url #> NULL #>  #> $remote #> NULL #>  #> $dispatcher #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $tls #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $port #> [1] 5555 #>  #> $stop_on_exit #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $within #> [1] "mirai" #>  #> $workers_within #> NULL #>  #> $chunks_per_job #> [1] 1 #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "parade_dist" # }  # SSH remotes (requires configuration) # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("mirai", quietly = TRUE)) {   dist_mirai(     remote = function() mirai::ssh_config(c("ssh://node1", "ssh://node2")),     dispatcher = TRUE   ) } #> $backend #> [1] "mirai" #>  #> $by #> character(0) #>  #> $n #> NULL #>  #> $url #> NULL #>  #> $remote #> function ()  #> mirai::ssh_config(c("ssh://node1", "ssh://node2")) #> <environment: 0x55d7f15cfd20> #>  #> $dispatcher #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $tls #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $port #> NULL #>  #> $stop_on_exit #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $within #> [1] "mirai" #>  #> $workers_within #> NULL #>  #> $chunks_per_job #> [1] 1 #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "parade_dist" # }

title: Create SLURM distribution specification — dist_slurm
text: Configure distributed execution SLURM clusters using batchtools. SLURM job runs one groups grid rows.

title: Create SLURM distribution specification — dist_slurm
code: dist_slurm(   by = NULL,   within = c("multisession", "multicore", "callr", "sequential"),   workers_within = NULL,   template = slurm_template(),   resources = list(),   chunks_per_job = 1L,   target_jobs = NULL,   callr_timeout = NULL )

title: Create SLURM distribution specification — dist_slurm
text: Character vector grid column names used define groups. unique combination columns becomes one group. Groups unit distribution — group assigned SLURM job. = "subject" 20 subjects → 20 groups → 20 SLURM jobs = c("shift", "ridge_x") → one group per shift×ridge_x combo = NULL (default) → every row group within execute work inside SLURM job, job running compute node. first three modes use furrr parallelise across rows; "callr" parallelises across groups, giving group independent R process. "sequential" (default): rows run one time. Use row already saturates node (e.g., script multi-threaded). "multicore": fork workers_within processes node. Good many independent, single-threaded rows multi-core node. "multisession": spawn workers_within R sub-sessions node. Like multicore works everywhere (including RStudio). "callr": group-level process pool. group runs independent R process via callr::r_bg(). workers_within processes run concurrently; one finishes next group starts (work-queue pattern). right choice want pack full node multiple processes use internal parallelism (e.g., script calls furrr, mclapply, relies threaded BLAS/OpenMP). Parade manages pool; script manages cores. workers_within Integer; many parallel workers ("multisession" / "multicore") concurrent R processes ("callr") run compute node. used "sequential". Defaults NULL, reads SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK runtime (.e., matches cpus_per_task resource request). template Path SLURM batch template file. Defaults parade's built-template (slurm_template()). Override custom preambles, module stacks, site-specific #SBATCH flags. resources Named list SLURM resource requests. passed batch template #SBATCH flags. Common keys: account, time, mem, cpus_per_task, nodes, partition. See Resource keys full list. chunks_per_job many groups pack SLURM job. Defaults 1 (one group per job). Increase reduce total number jobs many small groups. chunks_per_job = 1: 81 groups → 81 SLURM jobs chunks_per_job = 3: 81 groups → 27 SLURM jobs (3 groups ) target_jobs Integer; total number SLURM jobs create. Overrides chunks_per_job — parade divides groups evenly across many jobs. Useful fixed allocation want fill . target_jobs = 10 81 groups → 10 jobs (~8 groups ) target_jobs = NULL (default): use chunks_per_job instead callr_timeout Numeric; per-process timeout seconds within = "callr". callr worker alive longer , killed result () recovered. Useful safety net stage functions may leave background processes prevent clean exit. NULL (default) means timeout.

title: Create SLURM distribution specification — dist_slurm
text: parade_dist object SLURM execution

title: Create SLURM distribution specification — dist_slurm
text: Parade distributes work two layers: jobs (): grid split groups columns, groups packed SLURM jobs. job independent sbatch submission. Within job (within): rows inside job can run parallel using forked processes R sub-sessions. useful single SLURM job many cores (e.g., full node).

title: Create SLURM distribution specification — dist_slurm
text: Standard SLURM resources (passed #SBATCH flags): account, partition, qos, time, mem, nodes, ntasks, ntasks_per_node, cpus_per_task. Parade-specific resource keys: modules Character vector environment modules load compute nodes (e.g., c("StdEnv/2023", "r/4.4.0")). default parade captures modules loaded current R session replays nodes. Set modules = character(0) suppress module handling.

title: Create SLURM distribution specification — dist_slurm
code: # -- Basic: one SLURM job per subject, 2-hour wall time -- dist_slurm(   by = "subject",   resources = list(account = "my-account", time = "2:00:00", mem = "8G") ) #> $backend #> [1] "slurm" #>  #> $by #> [1] "subject" #>  #> $within #> [1] "multisession" #>  #> $workers_within #> NULL #>  #> $chunks_per_job #> [1] 1 #>  #> $target_jobs #> NULL #>  #> $callr_timeout #> NULL #>  #> $slurm #> $slurm$template #> [1] "/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/parade/batchtools/parade-slurm.tmpl" #>  #> $slurm$resources #> $slurm$resources$account #> [1] "my-account" #>  #> $slurm$resources$time #> [1] "2:00:00" #>  #> $slurm$resources$mem #> [1] "8G" #>  #>  #> $slurm$profile #> [1] "default" #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "parade_dist"  # -- Full-node jobs with within-node parallelism -- # Each SLURM job gets one full node (192 cores). Rows inside each # job run in parallel across those cores via forking. dist_slurm(   by = c("shift", "ridge_x", "ridge_b"),   within = "multicore",                # fork on the node   resources = list(     account       = "rrg-mylab",     time          = "8:00:00",     mem           = "0",               # 0 = all node memory     cpus_per_task = 192L,     nodes         = 1L   ) ) #> $backend #> [1] "slurm" #>  #> $by #> [1] "shift"   "ridge_x" "ridge_b" #>  #> $within #> [1] "multicore" #>  #> $workers_within #> NULL #>  #> $chunks_per_job #> [1] 1 #>  #> $target_jobs #> NULL #>  #> $callr_timeout #> NULL #>  #> $slurm #> $slurm$template #> [1] "/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/parade/batchtools/parade-slurm.tmpl" #>  #> $slurm$resources #> $slurm$resources$account #> [1] "rrg-mylab" #>  #> $slurm$resources$time #> [1] "8:00:00" #>  #> $slurm$resources$mem #> [1] "0" #>  #> $slurm$resources$cpus_per_task #> [1] 192 #>  #> $slurm$resources$nodes #> [1] 1 #>  #>  #> $slurm$profile #> [1] "default" #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "parade_dist"  # -- Sequential within (script is already multi-threaded) -- # Each job processes one group; the script itself uses all 192 cores. dist_slurm(   by = c("shift", "ridge_x", "ridge_b"),   within = "sequential",   resources = list(     account       = "rrg-mylab",     time          = "8:00:00",     cpus_per_task = 192L,     nodes         = 1L   ) ) #> $backend #> [1] "slurm" #>  #> $by #> [1] "shift"   "ridge_x" "ridge_b" #>  #> $within #> [1] "sequential" #>  #> $workers_within #> NULL #>  #> $chunks_per_job #> [1] 1 #>  #> $target_jobs #> NULL #>  #> $callr_timeout #> NULL #>  #> $slurm #> $slurm$template #> [1] "/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/parade/batchtools/parade-slurm.tmpl" #>  #> $slurm$resources #> $slurm$resources$account #> [1] "rrg-mylab" #>  #> $slurm$resources$time #> [1] "8:00:00" #>  #> $slurm$resources$cpus_per_task #> [1] 192 #>  #> $slurm$resources$nodes #> [1] 1 #>  #>  #> $slurm$profile #> [1] "default" #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "parade_dist"  # -- Reduce job count: pack 3 groups per job -- dist_slurm(   by = "subject",   chunks_per_job = 3L,   resources = list(time = "4:00:00") ) #> $backend #> [1] "slurm" #>  #> $by #> [1] "subject" #>  #> $within #> [1] "multisession" #>  #> $workers_within #> NULL #>  #> $chunks_per_job #> [1] 3 #>  #> $target_jobs #> NULL #>  #> $callr_timeout #> NULL #>  #> $slurm #> $slurm$template #> [1] "/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/parade/batchtools/parade-slurm.tmpl" #>  #> $slurm$resources #> $slurm$resources$time #> [1] "4:00:00" #>  #>  #> $slurm$profile #> [1] "default" #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "parade_dist"  # -- Fixed allocation: spread across exactly 10 jobs -- dist_slurm(   by = "subject",   target_jobs = 10L,   resources = list(time = "4:00:00") ) #> $backend #> [1] "slurm" #>  #> $by #> [1] "subject" #>  #> $within #> [1] "multisession" #>  #> $workers_within #> NULL #>  #> $chunks_per_job #> [1] 1 #>  #> $target_jobs #> [1] 10 #>  #> $callr_timeout #> NULL #>  #> $slurm #> $slurm$template #> [1] "/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/parade/batchtools/parade-slurm.tmpl" #>  #> $slurm$resources #> $slurm$resources$time #> [1] "4:00:00" #>  #>  #> $slurm$profile #> [1] "default" #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "parade_dist"  # -- Packed node with callr process pool -- # One SLURM job claims a full 196-core node. Parade runs up to 20 # independent R processes concurrently (work-queue). Each process # handles one subject group and is free to use its own internal # parallelism (e.g., furrr with 4 workers, threaded BLAS, etc.). # When a process finishes, the next group is launched automatically. dist_slurm(   by = "subject",   within = "callr",   workers_within = 20L,   target_jobs = 1L,   resources = list(     account       = "rrg-mylab",     time          = "12:00:00",     mem           = "0",     cpus_per_task = 196L,     nodes         = 1L   ) ) #> $backend #> [1] "slurm" #>  #> $by #> [1] "subject" #>  #> $within #> [1] "callr" #>  #> $workers_within #> [1] 20 #>  #> $chunks_per_job #> [1] 1 #>  #> $target_jobs #> [1] 1 #>  #> $callr_timeout #> NULL #>  #> $slurm #> $slurm$template #> [1] "/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/parade/batchtools/parade-slurm.tmpl" #>  #> $slurm$resources #> $slurm$resources$account #> [1] "rrg-mylab" #>  #> $slurm$resources$time #> [1] "12:00:00" #>  #> $slurm$resources$mem #> [1] "0" #>  #> $slurm$resources$cpus_per_task #> [1] 196 #>  #> $slurm$resources$nodes #> [1] 1 #>  #>  #> $slurm$profile #> [1] "default" #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "parade_dist"  # -- Explicit module loading (override auto-detection) -- dist_slurm(   by = "subject",   resources = list(     time    = "1:00:00",     modules = c("StdEnv/2023", "gcc/12.3", "r/4.4.0")   ) ) #> $backend #> [1] "slurm" #>  #> $by #> [1] "subject" #>  #> $within #> [1] "multisession" #>  #> $workers_within #> NULL #>  #> $chunks_per_job #> [1] 1 #>  #> $target_jobs #> NULL #>  #> $callr_timeout #> NULL #>  #> $slurm #> $slurm$template #> [1] "/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/parade/batchtools/parade-slurm.tmpl" #>  #> $slurm$resources #> $slurm$resources$time #> [1] "1:00:00" #>  #> $slurm$resources$modules #> [1] "StdEnv/2023" "gcc/12.3"    "r/4.4.0"     #>  #>  #> $slurm$profile #> [1] "default" #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "parade_dist"

what: Convenience: SLURM distribution from an allocation shape — dist_slurm_allocation
title: Convenience: SLURM distribution from an allocation shape — dist_slurm_allocation
text: allocation like "10 nodes × 196 cores", helper creates dist_slurm() specification :

title: Convenience: SLURM distribution from an allocation shape — dist_slurm_allocation
code: dist_slurm_allocation(   nodes,   cores_per_node,   by = NULL,   within = c("multisession", "multicore", "callr", "sequential"),   template = slurm_template(),   resources = list(),   target_jobs = NULL )

title: Convenience: SLURM distribution from an allocation shape — dist_slurm_allocation
text: nodes Integer; number nodes available concurrently. cores_per_node Integer; number CPU cores per node. Optional column names group parallelization. within Execution strategy within SLURM job: "multisession", "multicore", "callr", "sequential". template Path SLURM template file. resources Named list SLURM resource specifications (merged full-node defaults; user values win). target_jobs Optional integer; override default target_jobs = nodes (useful oversubscription, e.g., target_jobs = nodes * 2).

title: Convenience: SLURM distribution from an allocation shape — dist_slurm_allocation
text: parade_dist object suitable distribute().

title: Convenience: SLURM distribution from an allocation shape — dist_slurm_allocation
text: requests one node per SLURM job cpus_per_task = cores_per_node, uses target_jobs = nodes submit() creates (approximately) one job per node (optionally oversubscribed pass larger nodes), sets workers_within = cores_per_node default using within-job parallelism. still static partitioning submit time; highly heterogeneous task durations, consider oversubscribing (target_jobs > nodes) using dispatcher backend (e.g., dist_mirai() dispatcher dist_crew() cluster controller).

title: Convenience: SLURM distribution from an allocation shape — dist_slurm_allocation
code: # \donttest{ # Treat an allocation like "one big machine" (best-effort) dist_slurm_allocation(nodes = 10, cores_per_node = 196, within = "multicore") #> $backend #> [1] "slurm" #>  #> $by #> character(0) #>  #> $within #> [1] "multicore" #>  #> $workers_within #> [1] 196 #>  #> $chunks_per_job #> [1] 1 #>  #> $target_jobs #> [1] 10 #>  #> $callr_timeout #> NULL #>  #> $slurm #> $slurm$template #> [1] "/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/parade/batchtools/parade-slurm.tmpl" #>  #> $slurm$resources #> $slurm$resources$nodes #> [1] 1 #>  #> $slurm$resources$ntasks #> [1] 1 #>  #> $slurm$resources$cpus_per_task #> [1] 196 #>  #>  #> $slurm$profile #> [1] "default" #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "parade_dist"  # Oversubscribe to reduce stragglers dist_slurm_allocation(   nodes = 10,   cores_per_node = 196,   within = "multicore",   target_jobs = 20 ) #> $backend #> [1] "slurm" #>  #> $by #> character(0) #>  #> $within #> [1] "multicore" #>  #> $workers_within #> [1] 196 #>  #> $chunks_per_job #> [1] 1 #>  #> $target_jobs #> [1] 20 #>  #> $callr_timeout #> NULL #>  #> $slurm #> $slurm$template #> [1] "/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/parade/batchtools/parade-slurm.tmpl" #>  #> $slurm$resources #> $slurm$resources$nodes #> [1] 1 #>  #> $slurm$resources$ntasks #> [1] 1 #>  #> $slurm$resources$cpus_per_task #> [1] 196 #>  #>  #> $slurm$profile #> [1] "default" #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "parade_dist" # }

title: Convenience: SLURM distribution from a named profile — dist_slurm_profile
text: profile Profile identifier passed slurm_resources(). Typically character name registered via slurm_defaults_set() profile_register(); can also parade_profile object plain list. Optional column names group parallelization. within Execution strategy within SLURM job: "multisession", "multicore", "callr", "sequential". workers_within Number workers used within SLURM job. template Path SLURM template file. chunks_per_job Number groups process per SLURM job. target_jobs Optional integer; target number SLURM jobs create (overrides chunks_per_job submit time).

title: Create a parade flow for declarative data processing — flow
code: flow(   grid,   seed_col = NULL,   error = c("propagate", "keep", "omit", "stop"),   retries = 0L,   retry_backoff = c("none", "fixed", "linear", "exponential"),   retry_base = 0,   retry_on = NULL,   cancel = c("deps", "all", "none"),   resources = NULL,   cpus = NULL,   memory = NULL,   time = NULL )

title: Create a parade flow for declarative data processing — flow
text: grid data frame tibble containing parameter combinations seed_col Optional column name reproducible random seeds error Error handling policy: "propagate" (default), "keep", "omit", "stop" retries Flow-level retry attempts per stage (default 0). retry_backoff Backoff strategy retries: "none", "fixed", "linear", "exponential". retry_base Base delay seconds retry backoff. retry_on Optional retry classifier. NULL retries errors; character vector retries matching condition classes; function receives condition return TRUE/FALSE. cancel Cancellation propagation mode used error = "propagate": "deps" (default), "", "none". resources Optional flow-level resource defaults named list keys cpus, memory, time (aliases: cpus_per_task, ncpus, mem). cpus Optional flow-level default CPUs per task. memory Optional flow-level default memory (e.g. "8G"). time Optional flow-level default walltime (e.g. "2:00:00").

what: parade: Declarative Parallel Dataflow with Future/Furrr — parade-package
title: parade: Declarative Parallel Dataflow with Future/Furrr — parade-package
text: Declarative, lazy, compositional dataflow R top 'future'/'furrr', typed schemas, artifacts, diagnostics, HPC-friendly distribution (local, Slurm via 'future.batchtools'/'batchtools', mirai high-performance parallel execution). Adds ergonomic SLURM layer: generic script submission R, live CPU/memory monitors, multi-job dashboards, now site/project defaults NA--omit semantics profiles. Mirai backend provides unlimited parallelism, SSH tunneling, TLS security. Configure , run everywhere.

title: Unified dashboard for parade jobs — parade_dashboard
text: x parade_job, parade_jobset, list jobs. action One : "summary": print one-shot dashboard (default) "top": launch interactive text UI (delegates top()) "tail": show log tail (delegates tail.parade_jobset) "cancel_failed": cancel failed jobs (script jobs ) "collect_completed": collect results completed jobs refresh Refresh interval "top" (seconds). nlog Number log lines "top"/"tail". show_paths Whether print configured paths. show_artifacts Whether show small "latest artifacts" panel. artifacts_n many artifacts show show_artifacts = TRUE.

what: Initialize parade for HPC use — parade_init_hpc
title: Initialize parade for HPC use — parade_init_hpc
text: one-command setup helper running parade HPC clusters. function: Initializes path aliases "hpc" mode (prefer shared scratch node-local tmp). Optionally creates required directories runs quick doctor check. Optionally scaffolds batchtools SLURM template persists parade.json. Optionally sets SLURM defaults persists parade.json.

title: Initialize parade for HPC use — parade_init_hpc
code: parade_init_hpc(   scratch = NULL,   artifacts = NULL,   registry = NULL,   create = TRUE,   persist = TRUE,   template = TRUE,   template_path = "project://batchtools/parade-slurm.tmpl",   overwrite_template = FALSE,   slurm_defaults = NULL,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Initialize parade for HPC use — parade_init_hpc
text: scratch Optional shared scratch root. NULL, parade auto-detects using PARADE_SCRATCH / SCRATCH / scheduler variables. artifacts Optional artifacts root. Default file.path(scratch, "parade-artifacts"). registry Optional registry root. Default file.path(scratch, "parade-registry"). create Whether create missing directories configured roots. persist Whether persist paths/template/defaults parade.json. template Whether scaffold SLURM batchtools template file. template_path write template (default: "project://batchtools/parade-slurm.tmpl"). overwrite_template Whether overwrite existing template file. slurm_defaults Optional named list defaults SLURM resources (e.g., list(mem = NA, time = "2h")). quiet Whether suppress messages.

title: Initialize parade for HPC use — parade_init_hpc
text: list containing configured paths, config path (persisted), template path (created), doctor results.

title: Initialize parade for HPC use — parade_init_hpc
code: # \donttest{ # Minimal HPC init (best-effort autodetect) parade_init_hpc(quiet = TRUE, persist = FALSE, template = FALSE, create = FALSE)  # With explicit scratch + a temp template path (safe outside a cluster) parade_init_hpc(   scratch = tempdir(),   persist = FALSE,   create = FALSE,   template = TRUE,   template_path = tempfile(fileext = ".tmpl"),   overwrite_template = TRUE ) #> parade_init_hpc #> -------------- #> - Paths initialized (profile='hpc') #> - Project:   /home/runner/work/parade/parade/docs/reference #> - Scratch:   /tmp/RtmprbSKPY #> - Artifacts: /tmp/RtmprbSKPY/parade-artifacts #> - Registry:  /tmp/RtmprbSKPY/parade-registry #> - Template:  /tmp/RtmprbSKPY/file1b7c6bb422d6.tmpl #>  #> Warnings #> -------- #> - Missing directory for: data, config, cache  # }

title: Initialize parade path configuration — paths_init

Empty values treated unset.

what: Create a resource profile for SLURM jobs — profile
title: Create a resource profile for SLURM jobs — profile
text: Create fluent interface building SLURM resource specifications. Resource profiles can chained modifier functions build complex resource requirements readable way.

title: Create a resource profile for SLURM jobs — profile

title: Create a resource profile for SLURM jobs — profile
text: name Optional name profile (registry) base Base profile inherit (name profile object)

title: Create a resource profile for SLURM jobs — profile
text: resource profile object chaining methods

title: Create a resource profile for SLURM jobs — profile
code: # \donttest{ # Basic profile without pipes resources <- profile() resources <- res_time(resources, "4:00:00") resources <- mem(resources, "16G") resources <- cpus(resources, 8)  # Named profile for reuse gpu_profile <- profile("gpu_analysis") gpu_profile <- res_time(gpu_profile, "12:00:00") gpu_profile <- mem(gpu_profile, "64G") gpu_profile <- cpus(gpu_profile, 16) gpu_profile <- gpus(gpu_profile, 2)  # Inherit from existing profile and override time only extended <- profile(base = gpu_profile) extended <- res_time(extended, "24:00:00") # }

what: Resolve the full distribution plan for a flow — resolve_dist_plan
title: Resolve the full distribution plan for a flow — resolve_dist_plan

title: Resolve the full distribution plan for a flow — resolve_dist_plan
text: fl parade_flow object distribution settings.

title: Resolve the full distribution plan for a flow — resolve_dist_plan
text: parade_dist_plan list (print method) containing: backend Character — "local", "slurm", "mirai", "crew", "none". Character vector grouping columns. within Character — within-job execution mode. n_rows Integer — total grid rows. n_groups Integer — number groups. n_jobs Integer — number jobs/futures. groups_per_job Integer — groups packed job. workers List resolve_workers(). cores_per_worker Numeric — estimated cores available worker (meaningful callr). slurm_resources Named list SLURM resource flags, NULL. warnings Character vector potential issues.

title: Resolve the full distribution plan for a flow — resolve_dist_plan
text: conditional logic (parallelly availability, SLURM env vars, callr heuristics) resolved eagerly result shows exactly happen current machine.

title: Resolve the full distribution plan for a flow — resolve_dist_plan
code: grid <- data.frame(subject = paste0("sub", 1:40), x = seq_len(40)) fl <- flow(grid) |>   stage("sq", function(x) x^2, schema = returns(result = dbl())) |>   distribute(dist_local(by = "subject", within = "callr",                         workers_within = 10L)) resolve_dist_plan(fl) #> Distribution Plan #> ----------------- #>   Backend : local #>   Group by: subject (40 groups from 40 rows) #>   Jobs    : 40 (1 groups/job) #>   Within  : callr #>   Workers : 1 -- workers_within=10 but only 1 groups in chunk; pool capped at 1 #>   Cores/worker: ~4

code: # \donttest{ # A minimal local example (no SLURM required): # Create a job that fails once, then succeeds on retry. env <- new.env(parent = emptyenv()) env$i <- 0L flaky <- function(x) {   env$i <- env$i + 1L   if (env$i == 1L) stop("boom")   x + 1 } jobs <- slurm_call(   flaky,   x = 1,   engine = "local",   .as_jobset = TRUE,   .error_policy = on_error(action = "continue") ) jobs <- retry(   jobs,   policy = on_error_retry(times = 1, delay = 0, backoff = "none") ) jobs[[1]]$result #> [1] 2 # }

title: Get CPU and memory metrics for a SLURM job — script_metrics
text: function queries SLURM's accounting system retrieve job metrics: Data Sources: Uses sacct historical/completed job metrics Uses sstat live metrics running jobs Falls back squeue accounting unavailable Metrics Returned: cpu_pct: CPU utilization percentage ave_rss, max_rss: Average/maximum resident set size (bytes) ncpus/alloc_cpus: Number allocated CPUs elapsed: Elapsed time seconds node: Node list job running state: Current job state (RUNNING, COMPLETED, FAILED, etc.) Prerequisites: SLURM commands (sacct, sstat, squeue) must available PATH SLURM accounting must enabled detailed metrics Failure Behavior: Returns NA unavailable metrics (never errors) Warns SLURM commands missing Degrades gracefully accounting disabled

what: Add a script-based stage to a parade flow — script_stage
title: Add a script-based stage to a parade flow — script_stage
text: Wraps external script first-class pipeline stage declarative output file templates automatic parameter wiring. script expected write output files; script_stage() verifies exist returns file reference tibbles compatible artifact() / file_ref().

title: Add a script-based stage to a parade flow — script_stage
code: script_stage(   fl,   id,   script,   produces,   needs = character(),   engine = c("source", "system"),   interpreter = NULL,   prefix = TRUE,   skip_when = NULL,   skip_if_exists = FALSE,   use_manifest = skip_if_exists,   ... )

title: Add a script-based stage to a parade flow — script_stage
text: fl parade_flow object id Unique stage identifier (character) script Path script file (R, Python, bash, etc.) produces Character vector output declarations. Two forms: Templates (contain glue braces): glue-style path templates resolved per grid row. Can named unnamed (unnamed single defaults "output"). Names (braces): output names . script must call script_returns() declare actual paths. needs Character vector upstream stage IDs stage depends . source engine, upstream outputs injected script environment {stage}.{field} variables. engine Execution engine: "source" (default, uses base::source()) "system" (uses base::system2()). interpreter engine = "system", interpreter command. NULL, guessed script file extension. prefix Whether prefix output columns stage ID (default TRUE). skip_when Optional function (formula) receives row's variables (grid columns, upstream outputs, constants). returns TRUE, stage skipped row output columns filled NA. Useful avoiding redundant work outputs shared across multiple grid rows. skip_if_exists Logical (default FALSE). TRUE, checks whether resolved output files already exist running script. , script skipped valid file_ref() tibbles returned (written = FALSE, existed = TRUE). Requires template mode (produces glue placeholders); using manifest mode error. use_manifest Logical (defaults skip_if_exists). TRUE, enables completion manifest records {params} -> {output_paths} successful execution. subsequent runs manifest consulted first, allows skipping even produces template changed (e.g., adding new grid parameter). See completion_manifest(), manifest_adopt(), manifest_clear(). ... Additional constant arguments passed stage.

title: Add a script-based stage to a parade flow — script_stage
text: input flow new script stage appended.

title: Add a script-based stage to a parade flow — script_stage
text: Template mode — values contain glue placeholders (curly braces). script_stage() resolves paths, injects variables (output_path, <name>_path), verifies files script finishes: produces = c(model = "results/\{subject\}/model.rds") Manifest mode — values plain output names (braces). script decides write calls script_returns() declare paths. script_stage() reads manifest verifies: produces = c("model", "metrics")

title: Add a script-based stage to a parade flow — script_stage
text: Scripts can use get_arg() read parameters. works transparently source system engines:
code: x   <- get_arg("x") out <- get_arg("output_path")

title: Add a script-based stage to a parade flow — script_stage
text: Unlike skip_when, runs template resolution fills outputs NA, skip_if_exists runs inside wrapper paths resolved. means: Downstream stages receive real file_ref() tibbles can read. check uses actual resolved file paths, works correctly multiple grid rows map output files. declared output missing, script runs normally. skip_when skip_if_exists orthogonal can combined: skip_when evaluated first; skip, wrapper runs skip_if_exists checked next.

title: Add a script-based stage to a parade flow — script_stage
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Template mode: caller declares paths flow(grid) |>   script_stage("fit",     script = "scripts/fit_model.R",     produces = "results/{subject}/model.rds"   )  # Template mode: multiple named outputs flow(grid) |>   script_stage("fit",     script = "scripts/fit_model.R",     produces = c(       model   = "results/{subject}/model.rds",       metrics = "results/{subject}/metrics.csv"     )   )  # Manifest mode: script declares paths via script_returns() flow(grid) |>   script_stage("fit",     script = "scripts/fit_model.R",     produces = c("model", "metrics")   )  # System engine flow(grid) |>   script_stage("preproc",     script = "scripts/preprocess.py",     engine = "system",     produces = "output/{subject}.nii.gz"   ) } # }

title: Create a sink specification quickly — sink_quick
text: fields Character vector field names persist write Format name (e.g., "rds", "csv"), function, formula writing read Optional function formula reading (inferred format NULL) ext File extension including dot (e.g., ".csv"), inferred format NULL dir Base directory artifacts (default: "artifacts://_quick") template Glue template file paths (default: {.stage}/{.field}/{.row_key}) autoload Whether automatically load artifacts (default: FALSE quick sinks) overwrite Overwrite policy: "skip", "overwrite", "error" sidecar Sidecar metadata format: "json" "none" atomic Whether use atomic writes (temp rename) checksum Whether compute SHA256 checksums ... Additional arguments passed writer/reader functions

what: Create a temporary sink specification — sink_temp
title: Create a temporary sink specification — sink_temp
text: Like sink_quick() writes run-scoped temporary directory. Perfect testing ephemeral workflows need permanent storage.

title: Create a temporary sink specification — sink_temp
code: sink_temp(   fields,   write = "rds",   read = NULL,   ext = NULL,   prefix = "parade-quick",   template = "{.stage}/{.field}/{.row_key}",   autoload = FALSE,   overwrite = c("skip", "overwrite", "error"),   sidecar = c("json", "none"),   atomic = TRUE,   checksum = TRUE,   ... )

title: Create a temporary sink specification — sink_temp
text: fields Character vector field names persist write Format name (e.g., "rds", "csv"), function, formula writing read Optional function formula reading ext File extension including dot prefix Prefix temp directory (default: "parade-quick") template Glue template file paths autoload Whether automatically load artifacts overwrite Overwrite policy sidecar Sidecar metadata format atomic Whether use atomic writes checksum Whether compute checksums ... Additional arguments

title: Create a temporary sink specification — sink_temp
text: parade_sink specification object

title: Create a temporary sink specification — sink_temp
code: # Quick temp sink for testing tmp_sink <- sink_temp("result", write = "rds")  # CSV temp sink csv_tmp <- sink_temp("data",   write = ~ write.csv(.x, .path, row.names = FALSE),   ext = ".csv" )  # Will write to tempdir()/parade-quick-<runid>/...

what: Submit an R function to SLURM — slurm_call
title: Submit an R function to SLURM — slurm_call
text: Submits R function SLURM job serializing function arguments, executing compute node. provides ergonomic interface running functions SLURM without creating explicit script files.

title: Submit an R function to SLURM — slurm_call
code: slurm_call(   .f,   ...,   name = NULL,   name_by = NULL,   packages = character(),   resources = NULL,   template = NULL,   registry_dir = NULL,   env = character(),   lib_paths = .libPaths(),   rscript = file.path(R.home("bin"), "Rscript"),   write_result = NULL,   engine = c("slurm", "local"),   .as_jobset = FALSE,   .error_policy = NULL )

title: Submit an R function to SLURM — slurm_call
text: .f Function execute SLURM ... Arguments pass function name Optional job name (defaults "slurm-call") name_by Function string dynamic job naming. Can "stem", "index", "digest", function takes arguments returns string packages Character vector packages load compute node resources Named list SLURM resource specifications template Path SLURM template file (uses default NULL) registry_dir Directory batchtools registry (auto-generated NULL) env Named character vector environment variables set lib_paths Character vector library paths use rscript Path Rscript executable write_result Optional path save function result (e.g., "artifacts://result.rds") engine Execution engine: "slurm" (default) "local" debugging .as_jobset Logical indicating whether return single-element jobset instead bare job object. Defaults FALSE backward compatibility. .error_policy Error handling policy on_error(). Specifies handle job failures, including retry logic backoff strategies.

title: Submit an R function to SLURM — slurm_call
text: parade_script_job object monitoring job, parade_jobset containing job .as_jobset = TRUE. write_result specified, job object include result_path attribute resolved path result saved.

title: Submit an R function to SLURM — slurm_call
text: function works : Serializing function arguments RDS files Creating small runner script loads executes Submitting runner script via submit_slurm() function arguments serialized using saveRDS(), include function's closure environment. aware large objects captured closure can significantly increase serialization size. Packages specified packages argument loaded compute node executing function. function depends packages, either specify call library() within function .

title: Submit an R function to SLURM — slurm_call
code: # Local execution example (no SLURM required) local_job <- slurm_call(   function(x) x^2,   x = 10,   engine = "local" ) # Returns result immediately local_job$result #> [1] 100  # \donttest{ # Note: The following examples require a SLURM cluster environment if (Sys.which("squeue") != "") {   # Simple function submission   job <- slurm_call(     function(x) x^2,     x = 10,     name = "square-10"   )    # With packages and result saving   job <- slurm_call(     function(n) {       matrix(rnorm(n * n), nrow = n)     },     n = 1000,     packages = c("stats"),     write_result = "artifacts://random_matrix.rds",     resources = list(mem = "8G", time = "10min")   )    # Monitor the job   script_tail(job)   script_await(job)    # Load saved result   if (!is.null(job$result_path)) {     result <- readRDS(job$result_path)   } } # }

what: Plan packed chunking for a fixed-size SLURM cluster — slurm_cluster_plan
title: Plan packed chunking for a fixed-size SLURM cluster — slurm_cluster_plan
text: Parade (yet) provide true "SLURM pool" behaves like single machine nodes * cpus_per_node cores. However, can get close today using packed jobs (slurm_map(.packed = TRUE, ...)) choosing chunk size yields reasonable number SLURM jobs.

title: Plan packed chunking for a fixed-size SLURM cluster — slurm_cluster_plan
code: slurm_cluster_plan(   n_tasks,   nodes,   cpus_per_node,   oversubscribe = 2L,   nodes_per_job = 1L )

title: Plan packed chunking for a fixed-size SLURM cluster — slurm_cluster_plan
text: n_tasks Integer; number tasks map . nodes Integer; number nodes expect SLURM run concurrently (e.g., 10). cpus_per_node Integer; cores per node (e.g., 196). oversubscribe Integer; many jobs queue per concurrently usable node. Using oversubscribe > 1 helps mitigate stragglers heterogeneous task durations (SLURM start next job nodes free ). nodes_per_job Integer; number nodes packed job requests. Defaults 1.

title: Plan packed chunking for a fixed-size SLURM cluster — slurm_cluster_plan
text: named list : workers_per_node: recommended .workers_per_node target_jobs: recommended .target_jobs chunk_size: recommended .chunk_size (want pin ) resources: resource list can pass .resources

title: Plan packed chunking for a fixed-size SLURM cluster — slurm_cluster_plan
text: helper computes sensible packed configuration fixed cluster size known task count.

title: Plan packed chunking for a fixed-size SLURM cluster — slurm_cluster_plan
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ slurm_cluster_plan(n_tasks = 100, nodes = 4, cpus_per_node = 16) } # }

what: Map a function or script over elements via SLURM — slurm_map
title: Map a function or script over elements via SLURM — slurm_map
text: Submits multiple SLURM jobs mapping function script vector list. Automatically dispatches slurm_call functions submit_slurm scripts.

title: Map a function or script over elements via SLURM — slurm_map
code: slurm_map(   .x,   .f,   ...,   .args = NULL,   .name_by = "auto",   .resources = NULL,   .packages = character(),   .write_result = NULL,   .engine = c("slurm", "local"),   .progress = FALSE,   .options = NULL,   .error_policy = NULL,   .packed = FALSE,   .workers_per_node = NULL,   .chunk_size = NULL,   .target_jobs = NULL,   .parallel_backend = c("auto", "callr", "multicore", "multisession") )

title: Map a function or script over elements via SLURM — slurm_map
text: .x Vector list map .f Function, formula, script path apply element ... Additional arguments passed function script .args Named list additional arguments (alternative ...) .name_by Naming strategy: "auto", "index", "stem", "digest", function .resources Resource specification (profile name, profile object, list, NULL) .packages Character vector packages load (functions) .write_result Path template saving results (supports macros) .engine Execution engine: "slurm" (default) "local" .progress Show progress bar .options Flow control options (e.g., wave_policy() concurrency_limit()) .error_policy Error handling policy job failures .packed Logical; TRUE, pack multiple tasks single SLURM jobs efficient node utilization (default: FALSE) .workers_per_node Integer; number parallel workers per node packed (defaults resources$cpus_per_task present, else 1) .chunk_size Integer; number tasks per packed job (defaults .workers_per_node) .target_jobs Optional integer; .packed = TRUE .chunk_size provided, choose chunk size yields approximately many packed jobs (useful "treat N nodes like one machine" workflows). .parallel_backend Backend within-node parallelism .packed = TRUE. One : "callr", "multicore", "multisession", "auto". Ignored .packed = FALSE. Defaults "callr" strong isolation.

title: Map a function or script over elements via SLURM — slurm_map
text: parade_jobset object containing submitted jobs

title: Map a function or script over elements via SLURM — slurm_map
text: .f function formula (e.g., ~ .x + 1), element .x passed first argument function. .f character string path script, treated script submission appropriate argument conversion. .name_by parameter controls job naming: "auto": Automatic naming based context "index": Use numeric index (job-1, job-2, etc.) "stem": Extract stem file paths .x "digest": Use content hash function: Custom naming function receiving element index Packed Execution HPC Efficiency: Use .packed = TRUE pack multiple tasks single SLURM jobs better node utilization HPC systems. critical admins expect full-node allocations: Standard mode (.packed = FALSE): 1000 files → 1000 SLURM jobs → likely 1000 nodes Packed mode (.packed = TRUE, .workers_per_node = 20): 1000 files → 50 SLURM jobs → 50 nodes, using 20 cores Packed mode automatically: Chunks inputs batches Requests appropriate cpus_per_task Runs tasks parallel per node using selected backend (.parallel_backend): "callr" (default, isolated), "multicore" (HPC Linux), "multisession" Works flow control via .options (e.g., max_in_flight()) Preserves element-level naming result writing {stem}, {run} macros

title: Map a function or script over elements via SLURM — slurm_map
code: # Local execution example (no SLURM required) local_jobs <- slurm_map(1:3, ~ .x^2, .engine = "local") results <- collect(local_jobs)  # \donttest{ # Note: The following examples require a SLURM cluster environment if (Sys.which("squeue") != "") {   # Map a function over files   files <- c("data1.csv", "data2.csv")   process_data <- function() identity  # stub for example   jobs <- slurm_map(files, ~ read.csv(.x) |> process_data(),                     .name_by = "stem",                     .write_result = "results/{stem}.rds")    # Map a script with CLI arguments   jobs <- slurm_map(files, "scripts/process.R",                     .args = args_cli(verbose = TRUE))    # Use formula notation with SLURM   numbers <- 1:10   jobs <- slurm_map(numbers, ~ .x^2 + .x,                     .name_by = "index")    # PACKED EXECUTION: Process 1000 files using 20 cores per node   # This submits ~50 jobs instead of 1000, making HPC admins happy   files <- glob("data/*.csv")   jobs <- slurm_map(     files,     ~ read.csv(.x)[1:5, ],     .name_by = "stem",     .write_result = path$artifacts("results/{run}/{stem}.rds"),     .packed = TRUE,     .workers_per_node = 20,     .resources = list(cpus_per_task = 20, mem = "64G", time = "4h")   )   # Track progress and collect element-level results   results <- jobs |> progress() |> collect()  # Returns 1000 results    # Wait for all jobs and collect results   results <- jobs |> await() |> collect() } # }

One : "callr", "multicore", "multisession", "auto". Ignored .packed = FALSE. Defaults "callr" strong isolation.

title: Add a processing stage to a parade flow — stage
text: fl parade_flow object id Unique stage identifier (character) f Function execute stage needs Character vector stage IDs stage depends schema Schema defining expected output structure (returns()) prefix Whether prefix output columns stage ID (logical) sink Optional sink specification artifact persistence skip_when Optional function determine skip stage hoist_struct Whether hoist nested data structures (logical) inputs Optional character vector required input names stage. input_artifacts Optional character vector inputs must artifact/file-reference values. outputs Optional character vector declared output names. Defaults schema column names. io_mode /O contract behavior: "" (default), "warn", "error". retries Optional stage-level retry override (non-negative integer). retry_backoff Optional stage-level retry backoff override: "none", "fixed", "linear", "exponential". retry_base Optional stage-level retry base delay (seconds). retry_on Optional stage-level retry classifier override. resources Optional stage-level resource hints named list keys cpus, memory, time (aliases: cpus_per_task, ncpus, mem). cpus Optional stage-level CPUs per task hint. memory Optional stage-level memory hint. time Optional stage-level walltime hint. ... Additional constant arguments passed stage function

title: Submit a flow for deferred execution — submit

what: Submit an R script to SLURM or run locally — submit_slurm
title: Submit an R script to SLURM or run locally — submit_slurm
text: Submits R script SLURM job using batchtools runs locally, configurable resources environment. Returns handle monitoring retrieving results.

title: Submit an R script to SLURM or run locally — submit_slurm
code: submit_slurm(   script,   args = character(),   name = NULL,   engine = c("slurm", "local"),   template = NULL,   resources = NULL,   registry_dir = NULL,   env = character(),   lib_paths = .libPaths(),   rscript = file.path(R.home("bin"), "Rscript"),   wd = NULL,   .as_jobset = FALSE,   .error_policy = NULL )

title: Submit an R script to SLURM or run locally — submit_slurm
text: script Path R script file execute args Character vector command line arguments pass script name Optional job name (defaults script basename) engine Execution engine: "slurm" "local" (default: "slurm") template Path SLURM template file (uses default NULL) resources Named list SLURM resource specifications (ignored local) registry_dir Directory batchtools registry (auto-generated NULL) env Named character vector environment variables set lib_paths Character vector library paths use rscript Path Rscript executable wd Working directory script execution .as_jobset Logical indicating whether return single-element jobset instead bare job object. Defaults FALSE backward compatibility. .error_policy Error policy object retry logic (on_error())

title: Submit an R script to SLURM or run locally — submit_slurm
text: parade_script_job parade_local_job object monitoring job, parade_jobset containing job .as_jobset = TRUE.

title: Submit an R script to SLURM or run locally — submit_slurm
code: # Create a simple R script script_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".R") writeLines("cat('Hello from parade!')", script_path)  # Run locally (no SLURM required) job <- submit_slurm(script_path, engine = "local")  # \donttest{ # Submit to SLURM (only if available) if (Sys.which("squeue") != "") {   job <- submit_slurm(script_path, resources = list(time = "5min")) } # }

text: x parade_deferred object, collected results data.frame, parade_script_job, parade_jobset verbose Detail level: 0 (summary ), 1 (errors), 2 (full report suggestions logs) max_errors Maximum number individual errors display log_lines Number log tail lines show per failed chunk ... title: Diagnose pipeline failures — wtf

title: Diagnose pipeline failures — wtf
code: # \donttest{ grid <- data.frame(x = 1:4, group = rep(c("A", "B"), 2)) fl <- flow(grid) |>   stage("calc", function(x) x^2, schema = returns(result = dbl())) |>   distribute(dist_local(by = "group")) d <- submit(fl) deferred_await(d, timeout = 60) wtf(d) #>  #> ================================================================================  #> parade failure report #> Run: 5bf3bb4f  Backend: local  Submitted: 2026-03-30 16:39:45.738685 #> Stages: calc #> Elapsed: 0:00:03  Chunks: 2 total, 2 ok, 0 failed #>  #> No failures detected. #> ================================================================================  #>  # }
